paragraphs.text: Norwegian-born designer, working from Berlin since 2021.

Maren Solvik (b. 1988, Ålesund) is a graphic designer focused on brand identity
      and print work for cultural institutions. She studied visual communication at
      Oslo National Academy of the Arts (KHiO), graduating with distinction in 2012
      after a thesis on Norwegian municipal wayfinding systems.

For seven years she lived and worked in Bergen, first as a junior designer at
      a small studio serving regional museums and theatres, then as an independent
      designer working with publishers, festivals, and a long-running collaboration
      with Bergen Kunsthall. The west-coast years shaped her approach: slow research,
      careful typography, and a preference for printed matter that can be held.

Since 2021 she has run a one-person studio in Berlin, taking on a small number
      of identity, publication, and exhibition projects each year for clients across
      Northern Europe. She lectures occasionally at HAW Hamburg and is a member of
      Forening Norske Grafiske Designere. Outside the studio she keeps a Risograph,
      a stubborn sourdough, and a habit of walking the same canal route every morning.
Adobe InDesign CC — book and editorial layout
Adobe Illustrator CC — logotypes and mark-making
Glyphs 3 — custom letterforms and display type
Risograph printing — two- and three-colour editions

Adobe InDesign CC — book and editorial layout Adobe Illustrator CC — logotypes and mark-making Glyphs 3 — custom letterforms and display type Risograph printing — two- and three-colour editions Offset print production & press supervision Brand strategy workshops & stakeholder interviews Identity systems and design guidelines Exhibition graphics and environmental wayfinding Typography research & archival reference work Norwegian, English, and conversational German Currently reading The Politics of Design by Ruben Pater, alongside a stack of back issues of Slanted and the 1974 Norsk Designråd annual borrowed from a friend. © 2025 Maren Solvik.
